rationale: The cited paper is used as prior empirical evidence that contagion operates in consumer product contexts, specifically that disgusting products can taint nearby items. The citing paper repeatedly uses this point in its framing to define a research gap, arguing that prior contagion work (including Morales and Fitzsimons 2007) had not examined brand authenticity effects. This is more than a passing mention because it helps motivate the citing paper's core contribution and theoretical positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Morales and Fitzsimons (2007) by moving from disgust-based product contamination effects to a brand-level contagion account in which products from an original factory are seen as carrying brand essence. It preserves the contagion logic but applies it to authenticity and valuation judgments for branded goods rather than taint from disgusting contact.
